metadata.title: Nivolumab and FOLFOX-6 Modified Therapy
metadata.indication: First-line nivolumab with fluoropyrimidine/platinum chemotherapy for eligible advanced oesophageal, gastric, GEJ or oesophageal adenocarcinoma indications specified by NCCP.

clinical_governance.prescriptive_authority: The treatment plan must be initiated by a Consultant Medical Oncologist.
clinical_governance.dose_modification_note: Any dose modification should be discussed with a Consultant.
clinical_governance.disclaimer: JSON assessment encoding derived from NCCP 00209 Version 10a. Confirm the current official NCCP PDF and use independent clinical judgement. Clinical and oncology-pharmacy validation remain pending.

indications.description: First-line treatment of unresectable advanced, recurrent or metastatic OSCC with tumour-cell PD-L1 expression ≥1%.
First-line treatment of HER2-negative advanced/metastatic gastric, GEJ or oesophageal adenocarcinoma with PD-L1 CPS ≥5.

administration.route: IV infusion or SC injection according to NCCP

output_templates.contraindicated: The entered data trigger a protocol exclusion or contraindication pathway.
output_templates.discontinue: The entered data trigger protocol discontinuation.
output_templates.withhold: Hold treatment and follow the protocol reassessment pathway.
output_templates.consultant_review: Consultant review is required before treatment proceeds.
output_templates.dose_reduce: Treatment may proceed only after the encoded component modifications are reviewed and prescribed.
output_templates.proceed: No encoded dose-modification criterion was triggered by the entered data.

rule_engine.conflict_policy: Use the most restrictive overall action; combine component changes using the greatest reduction per component rather than summing reductions.
rule_engine.missing_data_policy: Evaluate only supplied values; do not infer missing values. Eligibility/context fields remain optional unless a specific rule requires them.

rules.explanation: Known hypersensitivity to oxaliplatin, 5-fluorouracil or an excipient is an exclusion.
Pregnancy and breastfeeding are protocol exclusions.
Peripheral neuropathy with functional impairment before cycle 1 is an exclusion.
Known complete DPD deficiency is an exclusion to fluoropyrimidine treatment in this regimen.

action.recommendation: Consider a reduced starting 5-FU dose with careful subsequent titration.

rules.explanation: Partial DPD deficiency requires individualised Consultant-directed fluoropyrimidine dosing.

action.recommendation: Confirm DPD status before treatment.

rules.explanation: DPD status has not been confirmed.
ANC remains below 1.5 ×10⁹/L or platelets below 75 ×10⁹/L after four delayed weeks.
The day-1 ANC or platelet threshold is not met; hold the complete regimen and repeat FBC weekly.
After count recovery, an ANC nadir of 0.5–0.99 ×10⁹/L requires a one-level oxaliplatin reduction.
After count recovery, an ANC nadir below 0.5 ×10⁹/L requires oxaliplatin reduction, omission of bolus 5-FU/folinic acid and one-level infusional 5-FU reduction.
After platelet recovery, a nadir of 10–49.9 ×10⁹/L requires a one-level oxaliplatin reduction.
After platelet recovery, a nadir below 10 ×10⁹/L requires a two-level oxaliplatin reduction.

action.recommendation: Consider 50% of the original oxaliplatin dose and haemodialyse within 1.5 hours after administration.

rules.explanation: Haemodialysis requires Consultant-directed consideration of 50% of the original oxaliplatin dose.

action.recommendation: Consider 50% of the original oxaliplatin dose.

rules.explanation: CrCl below 30 mL/min requires consideration of 50% of the original oxaliplatin dose.

action.recommendation: 5-FU is not recommended in severe hepatic impairment.

rules.explanation: 5-FU is not recommended in severe hepatic impairment; Consultant determination is required.

action.recommendation: The no-adjustment statement for mild/moderate hepatic impairment applies without renal impairment.

rules.explanation: Combined mild/moderate hepatic and severe renal impairment requires individualised review.
Grade 2 neuropathy at the start of a cycle requires a one-level oxaliplatin reduction.
The first or second occurrence of grade 3 neuropathy requires a one-level oxaliplatin reduction.
Persistent grade 3 neuropathy requires discontinuation of oxaliplatin.
Grade 4 neuropathy requires discontinuation of oxaliplatin.

action.recommendation: Increase the oxaliplatin infusion time from 2 to 6 hours.

rules.explanation: Laryngo-pharyngeal dysaesthesia requires prolongation of oxaliplatin infusion to 6 hours.

action.until: interstitial lung disease or pulmonary fibrosis is excluded

rules.explanation: Withhold oxaliplatin until interstitial lung disease or pulmonary fibrosis is excluded.
Diarrhoea remains grade 2 or greater after four weeks; discontinue treatment.
Grade 2 or greater diarrhoea on day 1 requires the complete regimen to be held and reviewed weekly.
After recovery from grade 3 diarrhoea, reduce bolus and infusional 5-FU by one dose level.
After recovery from grade 4 diarrhoea, reduce oxaliplatin, bolus 5-FU and infusional 5-FU by one dose level.
Stomatitis remains grade 2 or greater after two weeks; discontinue treatment.
Grade 2 or greater stomatitis on day 1 requires the complete regimen to be held and reviewed weekly.
After recovery from grade 3 stomatitis, reduce bolus and infusional 5-FU by one dose level.
After recovery from grade 4 stomatitis, reduce oxaliplatin, bolus 5-FU and infusional 5-FU by one dose level.

action.recommendation: Review local PPE policy and discuss any dose modification with a Consultant.

rules.explanation: Clinically significant PPE requires local-policy and Consultant review.
No encoded FOLFOX hold, discontinuation, Consultant-review or component-modification rule was triggered.

action.recommendation: Do not administer nivolumab; obtain consultant review.

rules.explanation: Relevant hypersensitivity is an exclusion.

action.recommendation: Do not proceed without urgent consultant review of pregnancy or breastfeeding risk.

rules.explanation: Pregnancy or breastfeeding requires specialist review.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab and treat/review the infection until clinically stable.

rules.explanation: Active clinically significant infection requiring therapy is an exclusion.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab until the infection is clinically stable.

rules.explanation: Grade 3–4 infection requires withholding until stable.

action.recommendation: Cycle 1 falls outside the encoded 42-day post-chemoradiotherapy window; obtain consultant review.

rules.explanation: Cycle 1 should commence within 42 days of completing chemoradiotherapy.

action.recommendation: Do not administer under this indication because progression after chemoradiotherapy was recorded.

rules.explanation: The indication requires no disease progression after definitive chemoradiotherapy.

action.recommendation: Use the protocol weight-based nivolumab schedule while body weight is 30 kg or less.

rules.explanation: Patients weighing 30 kg or less require weight-based dosing.

action.recommendation: Review the FBC abnormality clinically; no protocol-defined ANC or platelet hold threshold is encoded for single-agent nivolumab.

rules.explanation: FBC is monitored, but the NCCP protocol does not specify an ANC or platelet treatment threshold.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab and manage as immune-mediated pneumonitis.

rules.explanation: Grade 2 immune-mediated pneumonitis requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue nivolumab for grade 3–4 pneumonitis.

rules.explanation: Grade 3–4 immune-mediated pneumonitis requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab and manage as immune-mediated hepatitis.

rules.explanation: ALT/AST >3–5×ULN or bilirubin >1.5–3×ULN requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue nivolumab for severe immune-mediated hepatitis.

rules.explanation: ALT/AST >5×ULN, bilirubin >3×ULN, or concurrent transaminase and bilirubin elevation meets the permanent-discontinuation pathway.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab and manage the immune-mediated diarrhoea/colitis pathway.

rules.explanation: Grade 2–3 immune-mediated diarrhoea or colitis requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue nivolumab for grade 4 immune-mediated diarrhoea/colitis.

rules.explanation: Grade 4 immune-mediated diarrhoea or colitis requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab until clinically stable; treat hyperthyroidism/thyroiditis as indicated.

rules.explanation: Grade 2–4 immune-mediated hyperthyroidism or thyroiditis requires withholding until stable.

action.recommendation: Nivolumab dose change is not required solely for hypothyroidism; initiate thyroid replacement and confirm clinical stability.

rules.explanation: Immune-mediated hypothyroidism is treated with hormone replacement without a protocol dose change.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab until clinically stable and initiate corticosteroid/hormone replacement as indicated.

rules.explanation: Symptomatic adrenal insufficiency or hypophysitis requires withholding until stable.

action.recommendation: Endocrinopathy controlled on replacement therapy does not automatically require permanent discontinuation; confirm stability.

rules.explanation: Controlled endocrinopathies are an exception to the general permanent-discontinuation rule.

action.recommendation: Urgently assess and treat suspected immune-mediated diabetes with insulin as clinically indicated; the protocol does not require an automatic nivolumab dose change solely for controlled type 1 diabetes.

rules.explanation: Immune-mediated type 1 diabetes is managed with insulin without a protocol dose change.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab and manage as immune-mediated nephritis.

rules.explanation: Creatinine >1.5–3× baseline or ULN meets the grade 2 nephritis pathway.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue nivolumab for grade 3–4 immune-mediated nephritis.

rules.explanation: Creatinine >3× baseline or ULN meets the permanent-discontinuation pathway.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab for grade 2 rash lasting more than one week.

rules.explanation: Grade 2 immune-mediated rash lasting more than one week requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab and manage the grade 3 rash pathway.

rules.explanation: Grade 3 immune-mediated rash requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue nivolumab for grade 4 immune-mediated rash.

rules.explanation: Grade 4 immune-mediated rash requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue nivolumab and urgently manage suspected immune-mediated myocarditis.

rules.explanation: Grade 2–4 myocarditis requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab and manage immune-mediated myositis/polymyositis.

rules.explanation: Grade 2–3 myositis requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue nivolumab for grade 4 myositis.

rules.explanation: Grade 4 myositis requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue nivolumab and urgently manage the serious immune-mediated neurological syndrome.

rules.explanation: Serious neurological immune-mediated syndromes generally require permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Interrupt or slow the infusion; consider prophylaxis and close monitoring for subsequent doses.

rules.explanation: Grade 1–2 infusion reactions require interruption or slowing rather than an automatic permanent hold.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue nivolumab for a grade 3–4 infusion reaction.

rules.explanation: Grade 3–4 infusion reactions require permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab and follow the toxicity-specific immune-mediated pathway.

rules.explanation: Grade 3 other immune-mediated toxicity requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue nivolumab for grade 4 immune-mediated toxicity, except controlled endocrinopathies.

rules.explanation: Grade 4 immune-mediated toxicity generally requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue nivolumab for recurrent grade 3 immune-mediated toxicity.

rules.explanation: Recurrent grade 3 immune-mediated toxicity requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold nivolumab until the adverse reaction returns to grade 1 or baseline.

rules.explanation: Grade 2–3 non-immune adverse reactions require withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue nivolumab for a grade 4 non-immune adverse reaction unless the protocol exception is agreed by the consultant.

rules.explanation: Grade 4 non-immune adverse reactions generally require permanent discontinuation.

hypersensitivity.label: Known hypersensitivity to regimen component or excipient

baseline_functional_neuropathy.label: Baseline peripheral neuropathy with functional impairment

lowest_anc_delayed_weeks_x10e9_l.label: Lowest ANC during the delayed period

lowest_platelets_delayed_weeks_x10e9_l.label: Lowest platelets during the delayed period

crcl_ml_min.label: Creatinine clearance category

options.label: Dialysis - use the protocol-specific dialysis pathway / specialist review

crcl_ml_min.assessment_guidance: Select the protocol-specific renal band containing the verified CrCl/eGFR/GFR. Do not estimate a band without a valid renal-function calculation.

neuropathy_grade.label: Peripheral neuropathy grade
neuropathy_grade.assessment_guidance: Assess sensory and/or motor symptoms and their effect on instrumental versus self-care activities of daily living. Distinguish transient cold sensitivity from persistent functional neuropathy.

diarrhoea_current_grade.label: Current diarrhoea grade
diarrhoea_current_grade.assessment_guidance: Count stools per day above the patient's baseline, assess ostomy output, continence, hydration, fever, abdominal pain, haemodynamic compromise and whether hospital care is indicated.

diarrhoea_highest_grade.label: Highest diarrhoea grade since previous cycle
diarrhoea_highest_grade.assessment_guidance: Count stools per day above the patient's baseline, assess ostomy output, continence, hydration, fever, abdominal pain, haemodynamic compromise and whether hospital care is indicated.

stomatitis_current_grade.label: Current stomatitis grade
stomatitis_current_grade.assessment_guidance: Inspect the oral cavity and assess pain, ulceration, diet modification and whether oral intake is impaired.

stomatitis_highest_grade.label: Highest stomatitis grade since previous cycle
stomatitis_highest_grade.assessment_guidance: Inspect the oral cavity and assess pain, ulceration, diet modification and whether oral intake is impaired.

active_infection_requiring_therapy.label: Active clinically significant infection requiring therapy

infection_grade.label: Infection grade
infection_grade.assessment_guidance: Use the site-specific CTCAE infection term and grade according to local versus systemic treatment, IV antimicrobials, hospitalisation, organ dysfunction and life-threatening consequences.

pneumonitis_grade.label: Immune-mediated pneumonitis / interstitial lung disease grade
pneumonitis_grade.assessment_guidance: Assess new cough, dyspnoea, oxygen requirement, imaging findings, infection and alternative causes. Immune-related or drug-related pneumonitis requires urgent senior review when symptomatic.

diarrhoea_colitis_grade.label: Immune-mediated diarrhoea / colitis grade
diarrhoea_colitis_grade.assessment_guidance: Assess stool frequency above baseline and also assess abdominal pain, mucus/blood in stool and peritoneal signs. Grade diarrhoea and colitis separately using CTCAE v5.0, then enter the higher applicable grade and document which term determines it.

creatinine_ratio_baseline_or_uln.label: Serum creatinine relative to baseline or ULN

tsh_miu_l.label: TSH
tsh_miu_l.help: Interpret with free T4 and symptoms; no universal numeric hold threshold is encoded.

free_t4_pmol_l.label: Free T4
free_t4_pmol_l.help: Interpret with TSH and symptoms; no universal numeric hold threshold is encoded.

cortisol_nmol_l.label: Cortisol
cortisol_nmol_l.help: Interpret according to sampling time, symptoms and local endocrine guidance.

endocrine_status.label: Immune-mediated endocrine status
endocrine_status.help: Use the clinical syndrome and laboratory pattern together.

rash_grade.label: Immune-mediated rash / dermatitis grade
rash_grade.assessment_guidance: Assess morphology, body-surface area, symptoms, mucosal involvement, blistering, systemic features and effect on daily activities. Use the specific CTCAE skin term where possible.

rash_grade_2_over_one_week.label: Grade 2 rash lasting more than one week

myocarditis_grade.label: Immune-mediated myocarditis grade
myocarditis_grade.assessment_guidance: Assess symptoms at rest and with activity, troponin/other biomarkers, ECG, echocardiography or cardiac MRI as appropriate, treatment required and haemodynamic compromise. Suspected immune-mediated myocarditis requires urgent senior review.

myositis_grade.label: Immune-mediated myositis / polymyositis grade
myositis_grade.assessment_guidance: Assess pain, objective weakness, CK and other relevant investigations, swallowing or respiratory involvement, and impact on instrumental versus self-care activities. Suspected immune-mediated myositis requires urgent senior review.

serious_neurological_irae_grade.label: Serious immune-mediated neurological syndrome grade
serious_neurological_irae_grade.help: For syndromes such as myasthenia gravis, encephalitis, Guillain–Barré syndrome, myelitis or meningitis.
serious_neurological_irae_grade.assessment_guidance: Identify and document the exact neurological adverse event before grading. Assess objective deficit, instrumental versus self-care ADL limitation, bulbar/respiratory involvement and need for hospitalisation.

infusion_reaction_grade.label: Infusion-related reaction grade
infusion_reaction_grade.assessment_guidance: Document timing, symptoms, need to interrupt treatment, response to symptomatic therapy, recurrence, hospitalisation and any airway or haemodynamic compromise.

other_immune_toxicity_grade.label: Other immune-mediated toxicity grade
other_immune_toxicity_grade.assessment_guidance: Identify the actual adverse event first, then apply that toxicity-specific CTCAE definition. The generic severity anchors are not a substitute for the named CTCAE criterion.

other_nonimmune_toxicity_grade.label: Other non-immune adverse reaction grade
other_nonimmune_toxicity_grade.assessment_guidance: Identify the actual adverse event first, then apply that toxicity-specific CTCAE definition. The generic severity anchors are not a substitute for the named CTCAE criterion.

fbc_clinical_concern.label: FBC abnormality requiring clinical review
fbc_clinical_concern.help: FBC abnormalities are monitored but do not trigger a protocol-defined ANC/platelet hold unless the source protocol states a threshold.

weight_kg.label: Body weight
weight_kg.help: Patients weighing 30 kg or less require the protocol weight-based schedule.

supportive_care.mapping_basis: Highest active parenteral component is minimal emetogenic risk in NCCP V6 Table 7.
